sections.paragraph:
προσκλίνω 1 aor. pass. προσεκλίθην; plpf. 3 sg. pass. προσεκέκλιτο 2 Macc 14:24 (Hom. et al.; ins; TestSol 12:3 C; Jos., Ant. 5, 193) act. ‘cause to lean against’; in our lit. only pass. intr. to incline toward, with a view to association, attach oneself to, join someone w. dat. of pers. (cp. next entry; Sext. Emp., Math. 7, 324; schol. on Aristoph., Plut. 1027 τοῖς δικαίοις προσεκλίθη; 2 Macc 14:24) Ac 5:36 (s. also προσκολλάω); 1 Cl 47:4; 63:1.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

act. act. = active

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

intr. intr. = intransitive

dat. dat. = dative

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 881
